hypothesis: An equivariant graph neural network trained on the XDIP dataset to predict Fe K-edge XANES spectra from local atomic structure will achieve statistically significant lower prediction error (MAE reduction ≥15%) compared to the baseline GIN model reported in the paper, specifically for iron centers with coordination numbers ≥5, because incorporating SE(3)-equivariant representations of 3D atomic geometry will better capture the angular and bond-angle dependencies that dominate spectral features in higher-coordination iron sites—a relationship that can be directly tested by stratifying model performance by coordination number using the annotated structural properties already present in the XDIP dataset.
